title: USEC_DRVINFO
text: USEC_DRVINFO Determines the function codes that are valid for this driver. Expected Input Properties , Parameters Expected Output Flags1 Manipulated using macros. Return Values USEC_SUCCESS All initializations have successfully completed and the driver is ready to continue. USEC_ERROR Driver initialization failed Description Each security driver must supply information about the functionality that it intends to provide by setting a number of flags in the supplied structure. The flags indicate the function codes that are valid for the driver, and are checked before the function is actually called. This prevents unnecessary calls to security driver functions. The driver should perform any initialization or memory allocation it requires and use the member DrvArea to pass information between calls. The driver is responsible for releasing these resources. If USEC_ERROR is returned, the USecDrv structure is de-allocated. The driver must free its own allocated resources before returning. The returned error message must be a fixed string, as its scope must make it available to the caller. For the C language, macros are provided for setting and getting the the Flags1 member of the USecDrv driver structure. SMTP Security Drivers The parameters of the SetSecurity operation of the UPOPMAIL component are supplied in the Properties and Parameters members as input to this function. Related Links Security Driver Macros The USEC_DRVINFO function sets a number of bits in the Flags1 member of the supplied structure uSecDrv. In the C language, these must be set and read using macros. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
